NAME

server_protocol - Protocol Handler


- 📡 Network packet processing and protocol implementation

SYNOPSIS

Files


file protocol.c
📡 Server packet processor: client communication handling, protocol state management, and packet dispatch file protocol.h
Server packet processing and protocol implementation.

Functions


void handle_audio_opus_batch_packet (client_info_t *client, const void *data, size_t len)
Process AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH packet - efficient Opus-encoded audio batch from client. void handle_audio_opus_packet (client_info_t *client, const void *data, size_t len)
Process AUDIO_OPUS packet - decode single Opus frame from client.

Detailed Description

📡 Network packet processing and protocol implementation

Function Documentation

void handle_audio_opus_batch_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t len)

#include <src/server/protocol.c>

Process AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH packet - efficient Opus-encoded audio batch from client. Handles batched Opus-encoded audio frames sent by the client. This provides ~98% bandwidth reduction compared to raw PCM audio while maintaining excellent audio quality.

PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:

•
opus_batch_header_t (16 bytes):
•
sample_rate (4 bytes)
•
frame_duration (4 bytes)
•
frame_count (4 bytes)
•
reserved (4 bytes)
•
Opus encoded data (variable size, typically ~60 bytes/frame @ 24kbps)

PROCESSING FLOW:

1.
Parse batch header
2.
Decode each Opus frame back to PCM samples (960 samples/frame @ 48kHz)
3.
Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer

PERFORMANCE:

  • Bandwidth: ~60 bytes/frame vs ~3840 bytes/frame for raw PCM (64:1 compression)
  • Quality: Excellent at 24 kbps VOIP mode
  • Latency: 20ms frames for real-time audio

Parameters

client Client that sent the audio packet
data Packet payload data
len Packet payload length

See also

av_receive_audio_opus_batch() For Packet Types parsing

opus_codec_decode() For Opus decoding

handle_audio_batch_packet() For raw PCM Audio batch handling

Definition at line 1315 of file server/protocol.c.

1315                                                                                          {
1316   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "Received Opus audio batch from client %u (len=%zu)", client->client_id, len);
1317 
1318   VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1319   VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1320   VALIDATE_RESOURCE_INITIALIZED(client, client->opus_decoder, "Opus decoder");
1321 
1322   // Parse Opus batch packet
1323   const uint8_t *opus_data = NULL;
1324   size_t opus_size = 0;
1325   const uint16_t *frame_sizes = NULL;
1326   int sample_rate = 0;
1327   int frame_duration = 0;
1328   int frame_count = 0;
1329 
1330   asciichat_error_t result = packet_parse_opus_batch(data, len, &opus_data, &opus_size, &frame_sizes, &sample_rate,
1331                                                      &frame_duration, &frame_count);
1332 
1333   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1334     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Failed to parse AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH packet");
1335     return;
1336   }
1337 
1338   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, frame_count, "frame_count", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1339   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, opus_size, "opus_size", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1340 
1341   // Calculate samples per frame (20ms @ 48kHz = 960 samples)
1342   int samples_per_frame = (sample_rate * frame_duration) / 1000;
1343   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, samples_per_frame, 1, 4096, "samples_per_frame", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1344 
1345   // Use static buffer for common case to avoid malloc in hot path
1346   // Typical batches: 1-32 frames of 960 samples = up to 30,720 samples
1347   // Static buffer holds 32 frames @ 48kHz 20ms = 30,720 samples (120KB)
1348 #define OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES (32 * 960)
1349   static float static_decode_buffer[OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES];
1350 
1351   size_t total_samples = (size_t)samples_per_frame * (size_t)frame_count;
1352   float *decoded_samples;
1353   bool used_malloc = false;
1354 
1355   if (total_samples <= OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES) {
1356     decoded_samples = static_decode_buffer;
1357   } else {
1358     // Unusual large batch - fall back to malloc
1359     log_warn("Client %u: Large audio batch requires malloc (%zu samples)", client->client_id, total_samples);
1360     decoded_samples = SAFE_MALLOC(total_samples * sizeof(float), float *);
1361     if (!decoded_samples) {
1362       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for Opus decoded samples");
1363       return;
1364     }
1365     used_malloc = true;
1366   }
1367 
1368   // Decode each Opus frame using frame_sizes array
1369   int total_decoded = 0;
1370   size_t opus_offset = 0;
1371 
1372   for (int i = 0; i < frame_count; i++) {
1373     // Get exact frame size from frame_sizes array (convert from network byte order)
1374     size_t frame_size = (size_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(frame_sizes[i]);
1375 
1376     // DEBUG: Log the actual bytes of each Opus frame
1377     if (frame_size > 0) {
1378       log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Client %u: Opus frame %d: size=%zu, first_bytes=[0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x]",
1379                       client->client_id, i, frame_size, opus_data[opus_offset] & 0xFF,
1380                       frame_size > 1 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 1] & 0xFF) : 0,
1381                       frame_size > 2 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 2] & 0xFF) : 0,
1382                       frame_size > 3 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 3] & 0xFF) : 0);
1383     }
1384 
1385     if (opus_offset + frame_size > opus_size) {
1386       log_error("Client %u: Frame %d size overflow (offset=%zu, frame_size=%zu, total=%zu)", client->client_id, i + 1,
1387                 opus_offset, frame_size, opus_size);
1388       if (used_malloc) {
1389         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1390       }
1391       return;
1392     }
1393 
1394     // SECURITY: Bounds check before writing decoded samples to prevent buffer overflow
1395     // An attacker could send malicious Opus frames that decode to more samples than expected
1396     if ((size_t)total_decoded + (size_t)samples_per_frame > total_samples) {
1397       log_error("Client %u: Opus decode would overflow buffer (decoded=%d, frame_samples=%d, max=%zu)",
1398                 client->client_id, total_decoded, samples_per_frame, total_samples);
1399       if (used_malloc) {
1400         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1401       }
1402       return;
1403     }
1404 
1405     int decoded_count = opus_codec_decode((opus_codec_t *)client->opus_decoder, &opus_data[opus_offset], frame_size,
1406                                           &decoded_samples[total_decoded], samples_per_frame);
1407 
1408     if (decoded_count < 0) {
1409       log_error("Client %u: Opus decoding failed for frame %d/%d (size=%zu)", client->client_id, i + 1, frame_count,
1410                 frame_size);
1411       if (used_malloc) {
1412         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1413       }
1414       return;
1415     }
1416 
1417     total_decoded += decoded_count;
1418     opus_offset += frame_size;
1419   }
1420 
1421   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Client %u: Decoded %d Opus frames -> %d samples", client->client_id, frame_count,
1422                   total_decoded);
1423 
1424   // DEBUG: Log sample values to detect all-zero issue
1425   static int server_decode_count = 0;
1426   server_decode_count++;
1427   if (total_decoded > 0 && (server_decode_count <= 10 || server_decode_count % 100 == 0)) {
1428     float peak = 0.0f, rms = 0.0f;
1429     for (int i = 0; i < total_decoded && i < 100; i++) {
1430       float abs_val = fabsf(decoded_samples[i]);
1431       if (abs_val > peak)
1432         peak = abs_val;
1433       rms += decoded_samples[i] * decoded_samples[i];
1434     }
1435     rms = sqrtf(rms / (total_decoded > 100 ? 100 : total_decoded));
1436     // Log first 4 bytes of Opus data to compare with client encode
1437     log_info("SERVER OPUS DECODE #%d from client %u: decoded_rms=%.6f, opus_first4=[0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x]",
1438              server_decode_count, client->client_id, rms, opus_size > 0 ? opus_data[0] : 0,
1439              opus_size > 1 ? opus_data[1] : 0, opus_size > 2 ? opus_data[2] : 0, opus_size > 3 ? opus_data[3] : 0);
1440   }
1441 
1442   // Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer
1443   // Note: audio_ring_buffer_write returns error code, not sample count
1444   // Buffer overflow warnings are logged inside audio_ring_buffer_write if buffer is full
1445   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer && total_decoded > 0) {
1446     asciichat_error_t result = audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, decoded_samples, total_decoded);
1447     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1448       log_error("Client %u: Failed to write decoded audio to buffer: %d", client->client_id, result);
1449     }
1450   }
1451 
1452   if (used_malloc) {
1453     SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1454   }
1455 }




References audio_ring_buffer_write(),
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(), opus_codec_decode(),
    OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES, and
  packet_parse_opus_batch().






void
  handle_audio_opus_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


#include <src/server/protocol.c>


Process AUDIO_OPUS packet - decode single Opus frame from client.
    Handles single Opus-encoded audio frames sent by clients. The packet format
    includes a 16-byte header followed by the Opus-encoded data.




PACKET STRUCTURE:


    
  
  • Offset 0: sample_rate (uint32_t, native byte order - bug in client)
    • 
  
  • Offset 4: frame_duration (uint32_t, native byte order - bug in
    client)
    • 
  
  • Offset 8: reserved (8 bytes)
    • 
  
  • Offset 16: Opus-encoded audio data
    • 



Parameters


client Client info structure


data Packet payload


len Packet length


Definition at line 1475 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1475                                                                                    {
1476   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Received Opus audio from client %u (len=%zu)", client->client_id, len);
1477 
1478   if (VALIDATE_PACKET_NOT_NULL(client, data, "AUDIO_OPUS")) {
1479     return;
1480   }
1481 
1482   // Minimum size: 16-byte header + at least 1 byte of Opus data
1483   if (len < 17) {
1484     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS packet too small: %zu bytes", len);
1485     return;
1486   }
1487 
1488   if (!atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_audio)) {
1489     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS received before audio stream enabled");
1490     return;
1491   }
1492 
1493   if (!client->opus_decoder) {
1494     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Opus decoder not initialized");
1495     return;
1496   }
1497 
1498   // Parse header (16 bytes) - convert from network byte order
1499   const uint8_t *buf = (const uint8_t *)data;
1500   uint32_t sample_rate_net, frame_duration_net;
1501   memcpy(&sample_rate_net, buf, 4);
1502   memcpy(&frame_duration_net, buf + 4, 4);
1503   uint32_t sample_rate = NET_TO_HOST_U32(sample_rate_net);
1504   uint32_t frame_duration = NET_TO_HOST_U32(frame_duration_net);
1505 
1506   // Extract Opus data (after 16-byte header)
1507   const uint8_t *opus_data = buf + 16;
1508   size_t opus_size = len - 16;
1509 
1510   // Validate parameters
1511   if (sample_rate == 0 || sample_rate > 192000) {
1512     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid sample_rate: %u", sample_rate);
1513     return;
1514   }
1515 
1516   if (frame_duration == 0 || frame_duration > 120) {
1517     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid frame_duration: %u ms", frame_duration);
1518     return;
1519   }
1520 
1521   // Calculate expected samples per frame
1522   int samples_per_frame = (int)((sample_rate * frame_duration) / 1000);
1523   if (samples_per_frame <= 0 || samples_per_frame > 5760) { // Max 120ms @ 48kHz
1524     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid samples_per_frame: %d", samples_per_frame);
1525     return;
1526   }
1527 
1528   // Decode Opus frame
1529   float decoded_samples[5760]; // Max Opus frame size (120ms @ 48kHz)
1530   int decoded_count =
1531       opus_codec_decode((opus_codec_t *)client->opus_decoder, opus_data, opus_size, decoded_samples, samples_per_frame);
1532 
1533   if (decoded_count < 0) {
1534     log_error("Client %u: Opus decoding failed (size=%zu)", client->client_id, opus_size);
1535     return;
1536   }
1537 
1538   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_VERY_FAST, "Client %u: Decoded Opus frame -> %d samples", client->client_id, decoded_count);
1539 
1540   // Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer
1541   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer && decoded_count > 0) {
1542     asciichat_error_t write_result =
1543         audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, decoded_samples, decoded_count);
1544     if (write_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1545       log_error("Failed to write decoded Opus samples to buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(write_result));
1546     }
1547   }
1548 }






References atomic_load_bool(),
    audio_ring_buffer_write(), disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    and opus_codec_decode().
